member.name: Harriet Cross

addresses: Office 5/6, Thainstone Business Centre, Inverurie, AB51 5TB

member.synopsis: Harriet Cross is the Conservative MP for Gordon and Buchan , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. She currently undertakes the role of Opposition Assistant Whip (Commons).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, which of his Departmental projects will be (a) reduced and (b) ended due to departmental funding being reallocated to the defence budget; and how much they will be reduced by.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The savings are a small part of DESNZ’s overall budget, which saw a significant uplift at Spending Review. Our aim is for a large proportion of the savings to be found through efficiencies. We will set out more details in due course. The UK clean energy e...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: A96: Safety
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether she has made an assessment of the adequacy of road safety conditions on the A96 between Aberdeen and Inverness with regard to road lay out, traffic flows and vehicle types; and what steps she is taking
writtenQuestions.answerText: Road safety policy in Scotland is fully devolved, and responsibility for the operation, maintenance and safety of the A96 between Aberdeen and Inverness rests with the Scottish Government and its agency, Transport Scotland.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Meat Products: Origin Marking
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, if she will bring forward legislative proposals to amend food labelling regulations so that the Union Jack and 'British' country-of-origin labels can only be applied to meat products f
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK aims to maintain high standards on the information that is provided on food labels. All food sold on the UK market, including that which is imported, must comply with UK food labelling rules. The fundamental principle of our food labelling rules is...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Pigmeat: Prices
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether she plans to provide immediate financial assistance or supply chain interventions to support UK independent pig farmers following price changes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK Agriculture Market Monitoring Group (UKAMMG), formed by Defra and the Devolved Governments, has routinely followed the changes in pigmeat prices, including their recent downturn after several years of strong prices. The Department does not consider...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Cabinet Office: Publications
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, if he will publish a list of newspaper and media subscriptions paid for by his Department for (a) the Director of Communications, (b) the Press Secretary and (c) other staff based at 10 Downing Street by (i) pub
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the answer of 21 May 2026, Official Report, PQ 2387.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Veterans: Unemployment Benefits
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what representations he has made to the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions and other relevant bodies on having the War Pension Unemployability Supplement formally recognised as a qualifying benefit equivale
writtenQuestions.answerText: The status of the War Pension Unemployability Supplement as a qualifying benefit for passporting purposes is set through regulations by the relevant departments. The Ministry of Defence does not have the authority to amend this status and has not made for...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Pigmeat: Spain
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what assessment her Department has made of the potential economic impact on British independent pig farmers of Spanish pork imports being diverted into the UK market following Spain’s
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government routinely monitors imports of sensitive agricultural products for any unforeseen surges that could impact domestic industry. Imports from Spain currently account for 9.5% of UK pigmeat imports on a country-of-origin basis. The EU-UK Trade a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Wild Boar: African Swine Fever
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, with reference to the recent outbreak of African Swine Fever in wild boar in Spain, what steps her Department is taking to ensure that Spanish pork imports from outside the restricted
writtenQuestions.answerText: Commercial imports of pork and pork products from Spain into Great Britain must be accompanied by health certification signed by an official veterinarian, attesting compliance with animal health requirements to mitigate the risk of spread of African swine...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Pigs: Livestock Industry
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps her Department is taking to mandate equitable, legally binding, cost-of-production contracts between major supermarkets, processors and independent pig farmers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The ‘Fair Dealing Obligations (Pigs) Regulations’ aim to strengthen the pig sector by improving how contracts operate and giving farmers more control over how they sell their pigs. They introduce statutory requirements for written contracts between pig pr...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Veterans: Unemployment Benefits
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what his Department's policy is on ensuring that veterans in receipt of the War Pension Unemployability Supplement have access to the same passported benefits as recipients of equivalent Department for Work and P
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence (MOD) recognises the importance of supporting veterans in receipt of the War Pension Unemployability Supplement (UnSupp) and continues to work with relevant parties to ensure veterans receive appropriate support. However, responsib...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Nuclear Power: Scotland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment has been made, through Great British Energy – Nuclear’s siting study into new nuclear development in Scotland, of Scotland’s suitability to host both Small Modular Reactors an
writtenQuestions.answerText: In autumn 2025, Great British Energy – Nuclear (GBE-N) was commissioned by Department Ministers to assess Scotland’s potential for new nuclear power, whilst the government has also tasked GBE-N with identifying suitable sites that could host a potential l...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Heat Pumps
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, whether the target of 600,000 annual heat pump installations by 2028 will be met.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The target of installing 600,000 annual heat pump installations by 2028 was set by the previous government. This Government has put in place a package of policies – outlined in the Warm Homes Plan which was published earlier this year – that will help ens...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Taxation: Self-assessment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what estimate she has made of compliance costs incurred by businesses as a result of Making Tax Digital requirements.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the honourable member to the answer provided in the response to 122373. (5601) I refer the honourable member to the answer provided in the response to 123012. (5602) The National Infrastructure and Service Transformation Authority (NISTA) is expe...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Taxation: Self-assessment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, how much has been spent to date on the development and roll-out of Making Tax Digital; and what the projected cost is for completing that programme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the honourable member to the answer provided in the response to 122373. (5601) I refer the honourable member to the answer provided in the response to 123012. (5602) The National Infrastructure and Service Transformation Authority (NISTA) is expe...

writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the answer of 21 May 2026, Official Report, PQ 2387.

writtenQuestions.heading: Great British Energy: Staff
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, with reference to the Answer of 27 April 2026 to Question 128930 relating to Great British Energy staff numbers, how many people working for Great British Energy are employed as (a) permanent
writtenQuestions.answerText: (a) There are currently 30 permanent staff assigned to Great British Energy (GBE).(b) There are 37 temporarily assigned staff from the Department of Energy Security and Net Zero, with 21 staff on loan to GBE from other departments across government and 12...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Prime Minister: The Independent
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, whether the (a) Director of Communications, (b) Press Secretary and (c) other staff at 10 Downing Street had a work subscription to the Independent newspaper since July 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has access to a range of media outlets.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Aviation: Fuels
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what discussions she has had with the aviation sector on the (a) potential impact of jet fuel shortage on the industry and (b) distribution of available jet fuel between different parts of the aviation industry
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Secretary of State for Transport and departmental officials have engaged regularly with the aviation sector, including through fortnightly industry roundtables and bilateral discussions, to understand the potential impacts of disruption to jet fuel su...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Prime Minister: The Independent
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, whether any (a) Director of Communications, (b) Press Secretary and (c) other staff at 10 Downing Street has had a work subscription to the Independent newspaper since July 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the Hon Member in the time available before Prorogation.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Great British Energy: Staff
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, how many a) FTE roles there are at GB Energy b) GB Energy roles are permanently located in Aberdeen c) roles are solely GB Energy employees rather than seconded or equivalent from another Gov
writtenQuestions.answerText: There are currently over 120 people working for Great British Energy (GBE), including permanent, temporary and contingent labour staff. GBE is expected to recruit significantly more permanent roles in Aberdeen over the next financial year. GBE’s Aberdeen ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero
